content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates real elements of VIEW — particularly empathy and a willingness to be wrong (turn 6: 'You're right. I've been doing the thing where I keep reframing instead of answering. That's fair to call out.'), and by turns 12-14 the coach drops into a more genuinely curious, less agenda-driven stance, slowing down and matching the client's stillness. However, for much of the first half the coach is operating from a fixing/insight-delivering stance with clear agendas: stacked questions in turn 2 (three questions in a row, anxious not open), and clever reframes delivered as 'here's what's actually happening' in turns 4, 6, and 8 — exactly the trap of delivering voice-as-care as a clever insight. The coach has a clear preferred direction (get the client to see the belief underneath, get them to notice they shut the door themselves) and steers there. Wonder is more for content/diagnosis than for the phenomenon in front of them. Vulnerability is mostly absent — the coach never names their own state ('I notice I want to fix this') except once as a correction when called out. Pace matches the client's processing speed rather than slowing it. By turn 12 the coach finds a more genuine stance ('I just want to notice that you said it'), but the earlier territory was more strategic than open. Neither clearly 1 nor 3 — solid 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Stacked questions (3+ in one turn) signals anxiety/agenda rather than open curiosity. Reframe about wrestling match delivered as teaching."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Delivers voice-as-protector reframe as clever insight ('Can I share an observation') — exactly the trap. Has clear agenda about the belief gap."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Genuine undefended moment: 'You're right. I've been doing the thing where I keep reframing instead of answering.' Willing to be called out."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "But immediately pivots to a new agenda — the belief problem — and delivers another diagnostic frame. Still in fixing mode."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Stacks reframes and questions, in teaching/insight-delivery mode. 'That's actually just what intelligence is' is preacher-mode."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slows down. 'I want to be honest with you' — more genuine. Notices the client's pattern with real curiosity rather than diagnosis."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Drops into genuine VIEW — concedes the earlier point ('you're right, I should have been more precise'), refuses to lecture, asks one small specific question and waits."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds the moment, doesn't rush. 'That's not nothing' — undefended, present. Pace matches client's stillness."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["apply-view-to-the-voice", "voice-as-care"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "whats-the-case-against-it", "what-are-you-defending", "dont-fight-the-voice"],
  "notes": "The coach arrives at VIEW by turn 12 but spent the first half in strategic insight-delivery mode. The breakthrough in turn 15 happens partly despite, not because of, the earlier reframes — and partly because by turn 12 the coach finally stopped offering frames and got curious about the actual phenomenon (the bracing, the thirty seconds)."
}
